g.text: public.upgrade row lifecycle — discovery, execution, and recovery decision tree
The only way to know these paths actually work is to RUN them — the problem is too hard
to reason out. commit -> push -> CI builds the per-commit image -> run on a real VM ->
observe -> iterate. This diagram is the map (each case + its TEST coverage); the run is
exhausts → no longer transient → roll back → rolled_back
rolled_back — rollback succeeded; healthy on old version [auto]
(in_progress loop) — [HUMAN: unknown] waits for a person
chk_upgrade_state_attributes enforces the timestamp columns per state
(e.g. completed ⇒ completed_at NOT NULL, error NULL; rolled_back ⇒
rolled_back_at + error NOT NULL; failed ⇒ error NOT NULL, no rolled_back_at).
rolled_back = [auto] snapshot restored, healthy at the old version.
failed = [HUMAN: restore-broke] rollback's restore itself broke — box cannot reach runnable state.
(in_progress loop) = [HUMAN: unknown] unrecognised phase or error — waits for a person.
stays in_progress + loud, waiting for a person
OR persistent error (zero retries) → roll back
